mission.title: Install and Configure Containerssh on Kubernetes
mission.description: ContainerSSH is an SSH server that launches containers on demand in Kubernetes and Docker, providing dynamic access for various use cases such as lab environments, debugging, and honeypots. Installing ContainerSSH allows you to manage containerized environments efficiently and securely.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for ContainerSSH to isolate its resources.

steps.title: Deploy ContainerSSH
steps.description: Apply the ContainerSSH manifests to deploy the application.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description: Check that the ContainerSSH pods are running successfully.

uninstall.title: Remove ContainerSSH
uninstall.description: Delete the ContainerSSH resources and the namespace.

upgrade.title: Upgrade ContainerSSH
upgrade.description: To upgrade ContainerSSH, apply the updated manifest from the repository.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description: Check the pod logs for errors using: `kubectl logs <pod-name> -n containerssh`.

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description: Ensure that the service is correctly configured and check the service endpoints with: `kubectl get endpoints -n containerssh`.

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If pods are pending, verify that your cluster has enough resources by running: `kubectl describe nodes`.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of ContainerSSH will have all pods running in the 'containerssh' namespace, and you will be able to SSH into the service using the forwarded port. You can also verify that resource limits are correctly configured.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a working Kubernetes cluster and kubectl installed and configured to interact with it.
